sources.label: DIGITIMES - Inventec sees strong AI, general-purpose server demand through 2028 (May 12, 2026)
BigGo Finance - Inventec FY2026 Q1 earnings call: record NT$200.3B revenue, servers >50% of revenue (May 12, 2026)
BigGo Finance - Inventec chairman: 2026 AI server momentum to surpass last year, capex >US$1B

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at rank 14: no material news since June 6; the bullish data points (record Q1, >40% AI server growth, capex doubling) are mid-May vintage and already priced. A +58.5% YTD run with no fresh post-June-6 evidence trims residual alpha slightly on this crowding-sensitive list, but today's +2.0% drift does not change the setup from yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: Servers crossed 50% of revenue for the first time in a record Q1 (NT$200.3B, +27.6% YoY), AI server shipments guided to >40% growth in 2026, and >US$1B capex including a new North American base broadens the customer funnel beyond a $7.9B market cap.
verdicts.neutral: Solid AI-server EMS exposure with double-digit QoQ AI shipment growth guided for Q2, but the story is now well-telegraphed by management and sell-side; residual mispricing is moderate, consistent with its 'secondary residual alpha' bucket.
verdicts.bear: EMS assembly margins are thin and commoditized; >50% of AI server revenue from Chinese clients is a concentrated regulatory/geopolitical exposure, and a 58% YTD rerate leaves little cushion if China capex or B200-cycle orders wobble.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: May 2026 monthly revenue release (~June 10) and Q2 AI server shipment confirmation of guided double-digit QoQ growth.
verdicts.primaryRisk: China AI-server demand or export-control disruption hitting the >50% Chinese-client revenue mix, compressing the post-rerate multiple.
